rows.summary: OLD Item 17 consists solely of California-specific legal caveats about enforceability of certain provisions (e.g., choice-of-law, arbitration venue, non-compete, liquidated damages) and references to state statutes. NEW Item 17 replaces that with a comprehensive, structured, 23-point table detailing contractual rights and obligations regarding renewal, termination, transfer, post-term covenants, dispute resolution, and governing law — including newly specified cure periods, non-curable defaults, $250,000 liquidated damages for covenant breaches, 2-year post-term non-compete with 100-mile geographic scope, mandatory mediation/arbitration, and ACNA’s headquarters-based forum/law selection. This is a fundamental restructuring from passive legal warnings to active, detailed contractual terms.

rows.itemTitle: RENEWAL TERMINATION TRANSFER AND DISPUTE RESOLUTION
rows.summary: The NEW Item 17 introduces a formal, mandatory internal dispute resolution (IDR) process requiring franchisees to exhaust franchisor-led procedures—including travel to Omaha, NE—before mediation or arbitration. It restructures and expands the dispute resolution hierarchy: IDR → mediation → arbitration, with explicit venue (Bucks County, PA), administering body (AAA), and survival clause. The renewal requirements are substantively unchanged in substance but now explicitly begin with 'Subject to state law,' signaling heightened regulatory awareness. Liquidated damages and core termination/renewal terms remain identical.

All claims not subject to mediation or arbitration must only be brought in a competent court of general jurisdiction located in Bucks County, Pennsylvania

Franchisor and Franchisee agree that if this Agreement is terminated as a result of Franchisee's default before the expiration of the Term, it would be impossible to calculate with reasonable precision the losses that would be incurred by Franchisor

newEvidence.quote: You must first bring any claim that is between us to the attention of our management. You must first exhaust our internal dispute resolution procedures before you may bring your dispute before a third party

including, but not limited to, the requirement to travel to our headquarters in Omaha, NE for a meeting with the Chief Executive Officer and any other appropriate personnel. The requirement that you must first attempt to resolve disputes internally will survive the termination or expiration of your term.

Any and all disputes and claims that are not resolved by Internal Dispute Resolution must, be submitted to mediation, unless we provide in writing otherwise. The mediation will take place in Bucks County, Pennsylvania, under the auspices of the American Arbitration Association (“AAA”) in accordance with the AAA’s Commercial Mediation Rules then in effect.
